subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle v(t) = 2sin(e^(t/4))+1 — Speed Increasing, Avg Velocity, Total Distance & Position at Direction Change
stimulus: For $0 \le t \le 6$, a particle is moving along the $x$-axis. The particle's position, $x(t)$, is not explicitly given. The velocity of the particle is given by $v(t) = 2\sin(e^{t/4}) + 1$. The acceleration of the particle is given by $a(t) = \frac{1}{2}e^{t/4}\cos(e^{t/4})$ and $x(0) = 2$.

parts.prompt: Is the speed of the particle increasing or decreasing at time $t = 5.5$? Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct conclusion with reason: speed is increasing at $t = 5.5$ because velocity and acceleration have the same sign
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(5.5) \approx -0.453 < 0$ and $a(5.5) \approx -1.359 < 0$. Same sign (both negative), so speed is increasing.

parts.prompt: Find the average velocity of the particle for the time period $0 \le t \le 6$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up average velocity: $\frac{1}{6}\int_0^6 v(t)\, dt$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Average velocity $= \frac{1}{6}\int_0^6 v(t)\, dt \approx 1.949$.

parts.prompt: Find the total distance traveled by the particle from time $t = 0$ to $t = 6$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up total distance: $\int_0^6 |v(t)|\, dt$

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 6$, the particle changes direction exactly once. Find the position of the particle at that time.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Finds $v(t) = 0$: $t \approx 5.196$ (where $v$ changes from positive to negative)
1 pt: Sets up position integral: $x(b) = 2 + \int_0^b v(t)\, dt$ where $b \approx 5.196$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(t) = 0$ at $b \approx 5.196$. $x(b) = 2 + \int_0^b v(t)\, dt \approx 14.134$.
